coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Easy 45–50 minutes in Z2 (140–150 bpm), flat or rolling terrain. You're 67 days out and in taper week 20—volume is cut, intensity stays sharp. Yesterday's 11 miles at 124 bpm was solid aerobic work; today is active recovery to consolidate that adaptation. Skip the vert work and tempo; your body needs to consolidate the peak block you just finished. Keep it conversational, sub-150 bpm, and under 50 minutes.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: You logged 9.6 hours of sleep at 91% efficiency—excellent recovery signal. Eat 1.2–1.4g carbs per kg body weight today (spread across 4–5 meals) to replenish glycogen after last week's volume. Easy runs still require fuel: 200–250g carbs for the day, ~20g protein per meal, and salt to match your travel schedule. Alpine racing demands robust glycogen stores; don't skimp on carbs during taper just because volume is lower.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Your recovery score is 81/100 and sleep quality is strong. Maintain 8+ hours nightly through the remaining 9 weeks; this is your competitive edge over altitude and 2,500m of vert. No HRV data available, but resting HR at 63 bpm (only 1.4 bpm above your 30-day average) shows good autonomic balance. Stick to stretching, foam roll calves and quads for 10 minutes post-run, and prioritize sleep over any extra mobility work.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: You're in taper; expect mental fog and a false sense of detachment from fitness—normal. Use these 67 days to review the Julian Alps course map, study aid station locations, and plan your pacing strategy for the first 20K (where foot care and steady breathing matter most). Short, easy runs like today's are your chance to drill confidence without fatigue. Don't second-guess your fitness. You've earned this.
